sourceMovie.title: Breakdown
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Breakdown for the same reason it broke through in 1997: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jonathan Mostow keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gaslight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Breakdown clicked for you, Gaslight (1944) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Breakdown?
faq.answer: Try Gaslight, The Way of the Gun, Fargo—each shares DNA with Breakdown while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Breakdown?
faq.answer: Breakdown (1997) is best described as Crime, Mystery, Thriller, Drama, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Breakdown worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
